subject: [PATCH] mm: thp: pin the inode across a file folio split

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zi Yan suggested moving the EOF folio drop code to avoid a shmem_uncharge() issue, but agreed that explicit inode pinning is a better solution due to an implicit dependency on inode lifetime.

Gave Acked-by
reviews.raw_body:
I was thinking maybe we could move the EOF folio drop code in the unlock
loop to avoid the shmem_uncharge() issue you mentioned in the Closes.
But that hides this implicit dependency (I did not know about this inode
lifetime issue until this patch comes out). So I agree that an explicit
inode pinning is a much better solution.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that iput() is called while holding the folio lock, which could lead to a deadlock due to the interaction between the per-vswap spinlock and the folio lock in reclaim paths.
reviews.raw_body:
Sashiko is worried about iput() while holding folio_lock():

	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260713170915.239819-1-kirill@shutemov.name

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free bug in the THP (Transparent Huge Pages) split function by pinning the inode across a file folio split using igrab(). The problem occurs when a concurrent final iput() can evict and RCU-free the inode before __folio_split() is done touching the mapping. The patch introduces a new variable 'inode' to hold the inode reference, which is grabbed with igrab() before the split and dropped with iput() after the last mapping dereference. If the inode is already being evicted, the function returns -EBUSY.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a deadlock possibility if iput() drops the last reference and @lock_at is still in page cache, but expressed uncertainty about finding an obvious solution.
reviews.raw_body:
Sashiko is right. If iput() drops the last reference and
@lock_at is still in page cache we would self-deadlock.

I don't see an obvious solution. Will think more tomorrow.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-07-14.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free bug in the THP (Transparent Huge Pages) split function by pinning the inode across a file folio split using igrab(). The problem occurs when a concurrent final iput() can evict and RCU-free the inode before __folio_split() is done touching the mapping. The patch introduces a new variable 'inode' to hold the inode reference, which is grabbed with igrab() before the split and dropped with iput() after the last mapping dereference. If the inode is already being evicted, the function returns -EBUSY.
